mapping.a_element: strategic agents (principal and delegated proxy) with incentives in a mechanism
mapping.b_element: strategic traders (multiple insiders) with differing time-preferences in a market

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic economics/game-theory setting of strategic agents; the actual machinery differs. Paper A proves an algebraic identity (gain-from-misreport = within-range regret) and a trilemma impossibility result about when honest self-reporting to a proxy is optimal, whereas Paper B is an existence proof for a Radner equilibrium in a discrete-time Kyle insider-trading model plus long-run survival conditions, and no role-for-role correspondence links A's regret identity/guardrail trilemma to B's equilibrium-existence and survival analysis.
